channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan and Iran discuss US-Iran ceasefire and ongoing diplomatic engagement
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar held a telephone conversation with Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi to discuss recent regional developments, particularly focusing on the US-...

item.title: Taunsa Barrage Evictions Highlight Longstanding Issues with Boatpeople Community
item.description: In January 2026, a massive operation led by Punjab’s Pera force demolished Basti Shaikhan, a settlement of boatpeople, the Mohanas, located near Taunsa Barrage. These people, who have lived along the ...

item.title: Ramaphosa suspends Police Commissioner Masemola pending PFMA court case
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has placed National Police Commissioner General Fannie Masemola on precautionary suspension after his recent appearance in the Pretoria Magistrate’s Court. The move follows c...

item.title: Iranian embassy in Canberra investigated over alleged recruitment drive for war campaign
item.description: Iran’s embassy in Canberra is under scrutiny after promoting a recruitment initiative encouraging Iranians living in Australia to join a paramilitary campaign linked to the country’s war efforts. The ...

item.title: Samia Announces Roadmap for Justice, Reconciliation and Constitutional Reforms After 2025 Election Violence
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has presented a clear national roadmap aimed at restoring unity and addressing the aftermath of the October 2025 election-related violence in Tanzania. After receiving th...

item.title: Spain’s PM Defends Country's Commitment to NATO Amid US Threats of Expulsion
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez responded firmly to reports that the United States might try to expel Spain from NATO due to its refusal to support the US-led operations in the Middle East, parti...

item.title: Albanese reflects on Anzac Day, honouring sacrifice and reaffirming the value of peace
item.description: In this Anzac Day message, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese reflects on the enduring significance of the Anzac legacy and the sacrifices made by Australians in times of war. He highlights how, each yea...

item.title: Lessons for Canada from U.S.–Iran tensions on trade negotiations
item.description: The article analyses the implications of U.S.–Iran tensions for Canada's approach to negotiations with former President Donald Trump, particularly in the context of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agr...

item.title: Islamabad under indefinite lockdown as stalled US‑Iran peace talks drag on
item.description: The Pakistani capital, Islamabad, has been placed under an indefinite lockdown as the country mediates stalled US‑Iran peace talks aimed at ending a seven‑week war between Washington and Tehran. The c...

item.title: Thunder Dominate Suns, Face Injury Concerns with Jalen Williams
item.description: The NBA playoffs continued Wednesday night with a largely one-sided slate of games. The Oklahoma City Thunder extended their lead over the Phoenix Suns to 2-0 with a 120-107 victory, while the Detroit...

item.title: SERAP urges Tinubu to withdraw NBC directive restricting broadcasters' opinions
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) has asked President Bola Tinubu to immediately tell the National Broadcasting Commission (NBC) to cancel a recent directive it issued to br...

item.title: Inspekta Mwala Takes Legal Action Against Coca-Cola and Awinja Over Trademark Dispute
item.description: Veteran Kenyan actor, Davis Mwabili, famously known as Inspekta Mwala, has filed a lawsuit against Coca-Cola and actress Jacky Vike, popularly known as Awinja, over a trademark dispute. The lawsuit st...

item.title: Climbers delayed by unstable ice block near Everest base camp
item.description: A large ice block above Mount Everest's base camp has halted the progress of hundreds of climbers. The serac, a large block of ice between base camp and camp one, is unstable and considered too danger...

item.title: Nigerian Military Constitutes Court Martial to Try Personnel Over Alleged Coup Plot
item.description: The Defence Headquarters (DHQ) of Nigeria don set up a General Court Martial to try several military personnel wey dey linked to an alleged coup plot against President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. According to ...

item.title: Namibian Woman Reunites with Family in Homa Bay After 32-Year Search for Father
item.description: Aska Ndatekago Orlale from Namibia finally realised her dream of tracing her Kenyan roots after searching for her father for over 32 years. Her journey led her to Mbita in Homa Bay, where she discover...

item.title: Barry Cable Case Highlights Discrepancies Between Civil and Criminal Outcomes
item.description: In 2023, football legend Barry Cable was found by a civil court to have sexually abused a young girl in the 1960s and 1970s, leading to an $800,000 payout to the victim. However, a criminal trial on s...

item.title: PM Modi takes boat ride on Hooghly river during West Bengal Assembly elections
item.description: During the ongoing West Bengal Assembly elections, Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited Kolkata and took a boat ride along the Hooghly river. Emphasising the cultural and historical significance of th...

item.title: Kenya and UAE Presidents Discuss Trade, Security, and Regional Cooperation
item.description: During a working visit to the UAE, President William Ruto of Kenya met with Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the UAE President, to discuss strengthening bilateral relations. The two leaders focused...

item.title: CJP Afridi opposes proposal to transfer IHC judges, cites constitutional and institutional concerns
item.description: Chief Justice of Pakistan Yahya Afridi has refused a request from Islamabad High Court Chief Justice Sarfraz Dogar to convene a Judicial Commission meeting regarding the transfer of judges. In his wri...

item.title: Three Crucial Player Matchups to Watch in the 185th Soweto Derby
item.description: The 185th Soweto derby between Orlando Pirates and Kaizer Chiefs promises to be a pivotal match in the Betway Premiership, kicking off at FNB Stadium on Sunday at 3pm. Analysts have highlighted three ...
